failure_story: We sized American Airlines as a levered recovery stub at ~$15 per share, anchoring on mid-cycle operating cash flow near $4.70 per share and Lawrence/synthesis returns of 27–28% per year while the proof-first component schedule base case was −$9.79 per share and dhando was none. We treated ~$29 billion of debt and finance leases against $902 million of cash as a manageable deleveraging path funded by normalized owner cash, not as a refinancing cliff that could absorb equity first. Instead, a simultaneous earnings trough, fuel and labor cost spike, and credit-market tightening hit while capital spending already exceeded operating cash flow in FY2025 ($3.779 billion capex vs $3.099 billion OCF). Operating income fell further below the FY2025 $1.467 billion level, trailing operating cash flow per share breached the contract falsifier below $3.50 for four quarters, and maturing debt could not be rolled without dilutive equity issuance or covenant concessions. The market re-rated the stub from complexity-discount recovery option to distressed equity, and hold-period return landed far below the 15.2% bear Lawrence envelope—severe capital loss on a name with no moat, no bounded bear, and negative filing-locked component value at entry.
earliest_warning_signals: Proof-first component base −$9.79 per share vs price ~$15.14 implied paying a premium to filing-locked economic value before any recovery optionality.
Dhando none and payoff gates failed at packet freeze: durable cash flow (No), bounded bear at equity layer (No), cheap vs normalized (Marginal).
Net financial claims component base −$42.67 per share on ~$29 billion total debt and leases vs $902 million cash; equity was a thin stub on a levered balance sheet.
FY2025 operating income fell 44% YoY to $1.467 billion from $2.614 billion while revenue was flat near $54.6 billion—margin compression already underway at entry.
FY2025 capital spending $3.779 billion exceeded operating cash flow $3.099 billion; free cash flow was negative before debt service, not a deleveraging engine.

Lawrence base 27.0% and total synthesis 28.48% per year diverged sharply from proof-first component contract base annualized return (null at −$9.79/sh value); deep dive flagged Contract status evidence_blocked while valuation_contract.json read decision_grade.
Starting free cash flow per share in Lawrence path was $3.10 versus OCF proxy $4.70 per share—normalization inconsistency before any sizing.
AAdvantage co-brand option base $8 per share (band $0–$20) rested on judgment pending standalone loyalty disclosure; miles in ticket revenue were only partially separated from network OCF.
Cycle/leverage reserve base −$9.00 per share may understate fuel volatility and recession severity given high-yield OAS spread up 248.8% YoY (context, not in base IRR).
Insider conviction score 0.0 (negligible); Berkshire exited stake by 2020—no aligned insider signal supporting levered recovery thesis.
management_facts_2026-07-21.json has document_count zero; no primary management session on capex ROI or debt paydown plan.
Stance was watch with no dated catalyst, yet synthesis math displayed ~28% per year—complexity_discount narrative risked overriding gate failures.
Short activist scan was batch no_hit only on short_scan_2026-05-28.md; adversarial pass flagged Tier-1 web scan as [HUMAN REVIEW], not completed before any sizing.
forensic_checks: Reconcile quarterly operating cash flow per diluted share from 10-Q supplements against the contract falsifier (below $3.50 for four quarters); flag any stretch where OCF falls while total debt stays above $32 billion without proportional operating income recovery.
Extract full debt and finance-lease maturity schedule from FY2025 10-K debt footnotes; stress-refinance each tranche at higher spreads against $902 million cash and quarterly OCF run rate.

Trace Lawrence starting free cash flow ($3.10 per share) and mid-cycle owner-cash proof ($4.70 per share from $3,099M OCF ÷ 658.6M shares) to exact SEC locators; rebuild normalized owner cash from two-year average operating income $2,040.5M and document capex/maintenance split.

Sum four additive components (midcycle_passenger_network $33.88, aadvantage_co_brand_option $8.00, net_financial_claims −$42.67, cycle_leverage_reserve −$9.00) versus spot price and contract low/base/high value per share (−$41.70 / −$9.79 / +$26.15).

Separate AAdvantage co-brand and partner mile economics from consolidated ticket revenue and OCF each quarter; test whether co-brand option double-counts loyalty cash already in network capitalization multiple.

Build fuel and labor cost bridge from 10-K/10-Q MD&A versus operating income trend; model simultaneous fare compression and cost inflation per bear scenario (growth −5% years 1–5, exit 5× owner cash).
Compare actual capex to fleet renewal obligations and maintenance capital; verify whether growth capital and dilution were omitted per valuation_route failure_modes.
Monitor high-yield credit spreads, secured vs unsecured airline bond yields, and any 8-K refinancing or covenant amendment; quantify equity dilution if OCF cannot cover maturities.
Reverse DCF at entry ~$15.14: what operating cash flow per share and debt paydown path are required to deliver ~15% accumulate hurdle versus 28.48% synthesis display?
Run Tier-1 short activist registry and targeted forensic web scan; packet had no local short_reports/ and only batch no_hit on short_scan_2026-05-28.md.
Verify live price and diluted share count (658.621M from Q1 2026 net income $382M / EPS $0.58) at decision time against Yahoo close $15.14 (2026-07-20).
unresolved_items: Mid-cycle operating cash flow normalization ($4.70 per share OCF proxy vs $3.10 Lawrence starting FCF vs GAAP net income $111M) remains [Assumption] / [HUMAN REVIEW], not filing-proven deleveraging capacity.
AAdvantage co-brand milestone band $0–$20 per share lacks standalone SEC disclosure; overlap with ticket revenue OCF not fully audited.
valuation_contract.json status decision_grade conflicts with deep dive Contract status evidence_blocked and null component base annualized return—committee-grade pricing not reconciled.
Lawrence 27.0% / synthesis 28.48% per year vs proof-first base −$9.79 per share component sum undermines stance gate; synthesis weights remain human approval pending.
Full debt maturity wall, covenant headroom, and secured collateral coverage not modeled in additive build; net financial claims use filing-locked point-in-time balance only.
Cycle/leverage reserve (−$18 to −$3 per share band) may understate simultaneous recession, fuel spike, and credit tightening—the primary lens failure mode in the deep dive.
No management fieldwork or distribution policy on record (management_facts empty); deleveraging vs fleet capex priority unverified.
No approved third-party numeric source in base IRR; Berkshire SC 13G history is context tier only.
Disclosure scan was batch pass with no 8-K series review at packet freeze; refinancing or labor-agreement events may have been missed.
Bear scenario 15.2% per year was sensitivity only; dhando none means equity downside was not tightly bounded at sizing.
